text.title: CQRS Command Side
text.summary: Command base classes, PlaceOrderCommand and CancelOrderCommand handlers, Mediator for dispatch.

rule.statement: Commands return void or an ID — never a read model or DTO. A command changes state; state reading belongs to the query side.
rule.rationale: Returning data from a command forces the command handler to also build a read projection, coupling the two sides and preventing independent optimization.

rule.statement: Name commands in imperative voice: PlaceOrder, CancelOrder, ShipOrder. Each command has exactly one handler.
rule.rationale: One-to-one mapping makes routing explicit and predictable. Fan-out on the command side is a design smell — use domain events for fan-out.

example.note: Returns None. Domain events published after save. Order ID pre-assigned by caller (idempotent retry safety).

text.reference: Base Command + handler skeleton with full type hints
